patterns.name: AWS Access Key ID
patterns.notes: Standard AWS access key ID prefix. High signal, low false positive rate.

patterns.name: AWS Secret Access Key
patterns.notes: Requires the key label nearby to avoid matching random 40-char base64 strings.

patterns.name: GCP Service Account Private Key
patterns.notes: Matches the JSON key file signature GCP issues for service accounts.

patterns.name: Azure Storage Account Key
patterns.notes: Azure storage connection strings embed an 88-char base64 key.

patterns.name: OpenAI API Key
patterns.notes: Matches classic and newer sk- prefixed keys.

patterns.name: Anthropic API Key
patterns.notes: Anthropic keys use the sk-ant- prefix.

patterns.name: Stripe API Key
patterns.notes: Live secret keys grant direct payment/account access, treated as critical even though test keys are lower risk in practice.

patterns.name: GitHub Personal Access Token
patterns.notes: Covers ghp_, gho_, ghu_, ghs_, ghr_ token variants.

patterns.name: Slack Token
patterns.notes: Covers bot, app, user, legacy, and refresh Slack tokens.

patterns.name: Google API Key
patterns.notes: Standard Google API key format.

patterns.name: Generic API Key/Secret Assignment
patterns.notes: Catch-all for labeled secrets that don't match a known vendor format.

patterns.name: Private Key Block (PEM)
patterns.notes: Matches PEM-formatted private key headers across common key types.

patterns.name: JSON Web Token (JWT)
patterns.notes: Matches header.payload.signature structure.

patterns.name: Database Connection String with Credentials
patterns.notes: Matches URI-style connection strings that embed username:password inline.

patterns.name: Generic Password Assignment
patterns.notes: Expect this to need an allowlist for placeholder values like 'password' or 'changeme'.

patterns.name: US Social Security Number
patterns.notes: Severity sourced from Prudential's internal classification sheet (Pru_US_SSN_Full: Restricted/High).

patterns.name: Credit/Debit Card Number
patterns.notes: Severity sourced from Pru_Credit_Card_Number: Restricted/High. Paired with Luhn checksum in patterns.rs.

patterns.name: Bank Account Number
patterns.notes: Pru_Bank_Account_Number: Restricted/High, raw format is just 6-17 digits which is far too broad on its own, so this requires an 'account'/'acct' keyword nearby to cut down false positives.

patterns.name: ABA Routing Number
patterns.notes: Pru_ABA_Routing_Number: Restricted/High. 9 digits with a real checksum formula, validated separately in patterns.rs (not just shape).

patterns.name: US Passport Number
patterns.notes: Pru_US_Passport_Number_Full: Restricted/High, raw format is just 9 alphanumeric chars which is extremely broad, so a nearby 'passport' keyword is required.

patterns.name: US Employer Identification Number (EIN)
patterns.notes: Pru_US_Employer_Identification_Number_FULL: Restricted/High. Format XX-XXXXXXX.

patterns.name: US Individual Tax ID Number (ITIN)
patterns.notes: Pru_US_Individual_Tax_Identification_Number_Full: Restricted/High. ITINs always start with 9 and have a restricted range in the middle two digits per IRS spec, which keeps this from colliding with plain SSNs.

patterns.name: Alien Registration Number (A-Number)
patterns.notes: Pru_Alien_Registration_Number_Full: Restricted/High. Requiring the 'A' prefix keeps this from matching arbitrary 9-digit numbers, since a bare 9-digit number with no prefix is indistinguishable from many other IDs.

patterns.name: Email Address
patterns.notes: Pru_Email_Address: Confidential/High.

patterns.name: US Phone Number
patterns.notes: Pru_Phone_Number: Confidential/High.

patterns.name: Internal/Private IP Address
patterns.notes: Pru_IP_Address: Confidential/High. Private RFC 1918 ranges only.

patterns.name: MAC Address
patterns.notes: Pru_MAC_Address: Confidential/High. Standard colon or hyphen separated hex-pair format.

patterns.name: Internal/Non-Public URL
patterns.notes: Common internal TLD conventions.

patterns.name: Bearer Authorization Header
patterns.notes: Catches raw HTTP header dumps that include a live bearer token.

patterns.name: NPM Access Token
patterns.notes: Standard npm registry token format.
